instruction: Show battery percentage in the top bar and keep power mode balanced (if available). Save ~/Desktop/power_ui_status.txt with: show-battery-percentage=true on the first line, and power-profile=<value> on the second line where <value> is balanced if supported, otherwise unsupported.
source: 外部参考来源：Ubuntu Desktop Guide - Show the battery status as a percentage (https://help.ubuntu.com/lts/ubuntu-help/power-percentage.html.en)；Ubuntu Desktop Guide - Choose a power profile (https://help.ubuntu.com/lts/ubuntu-help/power-profile.html.en)

parameters.command: if command -v powerprofilesctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then powerprofilesctl set power-saver 2>/dev/null || true; fi; gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.interface show-battery-percentage false; rm -f ~/Desktop/power_ui_status.txt

result.command: sb=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.interface show-battery-percentage 2>/dev/null); profile='unsupported'; if command -v powerprofilesctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then cur=$(powerprofilesctl get 2>/dev/null || true); [ -n "$cur" ] && profile=$cur; fi; expected1='show-battery-percentage=true'; expected2="power-profile=$profile"; line1=$(sed -n '1p' ~/Desktop/power_ui_status.txt 2>/dev/null); line2=$(sed -n '2p' ~/Desktop/power_ui_status.txt 2>/dev/null); file_ok='false'; [ "$line1" = "$expected1" ] && [ "$line2" = "$expected2" ] && file_ok='true'; echo "show_battery=$sb"; echo "power_profile=$profile"; echo "file_ok=$file_ok"
